HAIL WAS ONE TO TWO INCHES DEEP AND THE LARGEST STONES WERE ONE INCH IN DIAMETER
A FOUR INCH DIAMETER ASPEN TREE WAS SNAPPED OFF MIDWAY UP. DID NOT HIT ANYTHING OR CAUSE OTHER DAMAGE
OVER ONE HUNDRED TREES DOWN BETWEEN 7 AND 14 MILES NORTH OF SEELEY LAKE.
MULTIPLE TREES BLOWN DOWN ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF MISSOULA. ONE TREE WAS 10 TO 12 INCHES IN DIAMETER AND WAS SNAPPED OFF AT THE BASE.
TREES COVEREING HIGHWAY MT-83 NEAR MILE POST 48.
ROCK SLIDE NEAR RISING SUN AND A BOULDER BLOCKING EASTBOUND TRAFFIC NEAR LOGAN PASS DUE TO HEAVY RAIN.
DEBRIS FLOW INTO THE SALMON RIVER FOLLOWING HEAVY RAIN. ALSO REPORTED HAIL THE SIZE OF DIMES.
20 REPORTS OF WIND DAMAGE...TREES DOWN ON POWER LINES AND HOMES. ALSO 2115 PEOPLE WERE WITHOUT POWER FOR A FEW HOURS.

SALMON RAWS AT 5100 FT

FOREST SERVICE REPORTED 20 TREES DOWN BLOCKING THE SERVICE ROAD TO THE DORR SKEELS CAMPGROUND AT AROUND 230 AM. TIME OF POTENTIAL DOWNBURST COULD HAVE BEEN BETWEEN 100 AND 130 AM MDT ACCORDING TO RADAR.

LARGE ENOUGH THAT DOT WAS CALLED IN TO REMOVE ROCK SLIDE AT MILEMARKER 221 ON HIGHWAY 95.

PUBLIC REPORTED QUARTER SIZE HAIL THAT COVERED THE GROUND UP TO 1.5 INCHES DEEP.
